# Mutation-testing equivalence file
#
# Documented-equivalent mutations to suppress from the survivor count.
# Each non-blank, non-comment line:
#
#     <file>:<line>:<op>:<original>:<mutated>    # optional comment
#
# Match is exact on all five fields, except line may be M-N for a range
# and op/original/mutated may each be `*` to match anything. When a
# mutant's fingerprint matches, it's reported as IGNORED rather than
# SURVIVED, and the effective kill rate is computed as
# killed / (total - ignored).
#
# Adding entries is a deliberate act:
#   - Verify the mutation is *truly* equivalent (no observable behaviour
#     change for any reasonable input the function sees).
#   - Cite the equivalence class in the trailing comment so the next
#     reviewer can re-verify.
#
# Equivalence classes (extend in docs/MUTATION-TESTING.md):
#   OPT  - early-return optimisation guard (correctness unaffected;
#          mutation only causes wasted work)
#   WGT  - composite-score weight constant where the literal ±1
#          perturbation can't shift bucketed output
#   LZT  - loop bound reading zero-initialised tail (e.g. unused
#          char_counts[] slots)
#   FAB  - function/local-as-boolean: any non-zero replacement is
#          still truthy and observably equivalent
#   ALG  - allocator-internal sizing/growth literal that changes how
#          memory is acquired but not the algorithm's output
#   SAN  - sanitizer-detectable memory bug (buffer overrun, read-past-
#          end, free-of-uninitialised). Survives our -O0 test build
#          because macOS's allocator doesn't fault; would be killed by
#          a re-run with `make CFLAGS='-O0 -g -fsanitize=address,undefined'`
#          (see docs/MUTATION-TESTING.md for the ASAN run target)
#   IO   - mutation lives inside an IO/network/interactive code path
#          (popen/curl, fread loop, getchar prompt, system() install).
#          Killing it requires popen-mocking or a test-harness
#          libcurl substitute. Out of scope for the unit suite;
#          documented so the survivor count reflects what the unit
#          tests CAN reach.
#   DCB  - dead code branch: mutation lives in code that the test
#          build's #ifdef path doesn't compile (e.g. the production
#          arc4random / getrandom paths in src/random.c are excluded
#          under -DMEOW_TEST_RNG, so mutations there produce a
#          binary identical to the unmutated one). Killing requires
#          a separate mutate pass without -DMEOW_TEST_RNG against the
#          production build, which is outside this harness's default.
#   DRC  - dead reachable code: the branch IS compiled, but no caller
#          ever drives it down the mutated path (e.g. the >8-byte
#          chunk arithmetic in the test PRNG, where the only caller
#          requests 4 bytes).
#
# Start empty. Earn each entry by triaging a real survivor.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/complexity.c — Round 1 triage
# ------------------------------------------------------------
src/complexity.c:34:rel:<:<=          # LZT: char_counts[256] is read past array end; the >0 guard at L35 makes any value at that index unobservable (UB-tolerant on typical stacks)
src/complexity.c:52:const:1:0         # OPT: run_length init is overwritten on the first loop iteration's else branch (i=0 always differs from '\0')
src/complexity.c:167:const:10:11      # WGT: score cap unreachable — max composite is ~3.1 given the current 0.3/0.25/0.2/0.15/0.1 weights and component ranges
src/complexity.c:167:const:10:9       # WGT: same — even a 9.0 cap exceeds the max achievable composite

# Round 2 — pattern_complexity equivalents
src/complexity.c:76:const:2:1         # OPT: `if (len < 2)` → `if (len < 1)`. For len=1 the outer loop never runs (slen starts at 2 > 1) so both paths return 0.0 via the total_substrings==0 guard at L127.
src/complexity.c:93:const:1:2         # FAB: `int is_unique = 1` used purely as boolean (only checked via `if (is_unique)`); any non-zero value is observably identical.
src/complexity.c:105:const:64:65      # ALG: initial substr_capacity literal; affects realloc cadence, not output.
src/complexity.c:105:const:64:63      # ALG: same.
src/complexity.c:105:const:2:3        # ALG: realloc growth factor (2x → 3x); changes capacity progression, not unique-substring count.
src/complexity.c:110:const:1:2        # ALG: `malloc(slen + 1)` → `malloc(slen + 2)`; one extra byte is harmless tail.
src/complexity.c:110:arith:+:-        # SAN: `malloc(slen + 1)` → `malloc(slen - 1)`; heap overflow on the strncpy+null-terminator that follows. Killed by ASAN.
src/complexity.c:110:const:1:0        # SAN: `malloc(slen + 1)` → `malloc(slen)`; one-byte overflow on the null terminator. Killed by ASAN.
src/complexity.c:122:rel:<:<=         # SAN: free-loop `i < substr_count` → `i <= substr_count`; frees one uninitialised slot past the populated range. Killed by ASAN.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/password.c — Round 2 triage
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# select_and_join_names guards — count=0 / names_count=0 short-circuits
# fall through to no-op loops; observable output is unchanged.
src/password.c:67:rel:<=:<            # OPT: `count <= 0` → `count < 0`; for count=0 the function falls through but actual_count=0 makes both loops no-op.
src/password.c:67:bool:||:&&          # OPT: same as above via AND-of-guards path.
src/password.c:72:rel:>:>=            # OPT: tiebreak `count > names_count` → `>=`; at equality both branches yield the same actual_count value.
src/password.c:76:rel:>:>=            # OPT: tiebreak `actual_count+5 > names_count` → `>=`; with names_count=16913 and actual_count<=6 never equal in practice.
src/password.c:79:rel:>:>=            # OPT: tiebreak `total_picks > sizeof(indices)`; at equality the cap is a no-op.
src/password.c:81:rel:>:>=            # OPT: tiebreak `actual_count > total_picks`; at equality the assignment is a no-op.
src/password.c:98:rel:>:>=            # OPT: tiebreak `strlen > max_length`; at equality the truncation writes the same '\0' position.
# randomly_capitalize guards
src/password.c:105:rel:<=:<           # OPT: `count <= 0` → `count < 0`; for count=0 to_cap=0 makes the loop no-op (same observable as the early return).
src/password.c:105:bool:||:&&         # OPT: same — for len=0 letter_count=0 path returns early; AND-of-guards still hits the early-return in the no-letters case.
src/password.c:112:rel:<:<=           # LZT: `i < len` → `i <= len`; reads '\0' which fails isalpha so letter_indices is unchanged.
src/password.c:125:rel:>:>=           # OPT: tiebreak `count > letter_count`; at equality both branches yield same to_cap.
# replace_with_symbols guards (mirror of randomly_capitalize)
src/password.c:153:rel:<=:<           # OPT: count=0 path falls through to no-op.
src/password.c:153:bool:||:&&         # OPT: same.
src/password.c:162:rel:<:<=           # LZT: same '\0' tail read as L112.
src/password.c:175:rel:>:>=           # OPT: tiebreak.
# Allocator / sizing literals — affect memory cadence not output
src/password.c:78:const:16:17         # ALG: `size_t indices[16]` larger array; harmless tail.
src/password.c:78:const:16:15         # ALG: smaller array; total_picks cap at L79 ensures no overrun in practice (max needed is 11).
# Boolean perturbation
src/password.c:41:const:1:2           # FAB: `dup = 1` used purely as bool; any non-zero is observably identical.
# Sanitizer-class memory bugs — real, but silent without ASAN
src/password.c:37:rel:<:<=            # SAN: pick_random_indices writes past out[k-1] by one slot; killed by ASAN.
src/password.c:40:rel:<:<=            # SAN: reads uninitialised out[chosen] in dup-detect; killed by ASAN/MSAN.
src/password.c:51:rel:<:<=            # SAN: append_name writes one byte past output[max_length-1].
src/password.c:51:arith:-:+           # SAN: `max_length - 1` → `+ 1` writes two bytes past.
src/password.c:51:const:1:0           # SAN: `max_length - 1` → `max_length` writes one byte past.
src/password.c:89:rel:<:<=            # SAN: loop bound `out_len < max_length-1` overrun.
src/password.c:89:arith:-:+           # SAN: same line, second target.
src/password.c:89:bool:&&:||          # SAN: OR-of-guards lets loop continue past capacity check.
src/password.c:94:rel:<:<=            # SAN: extras loop overrun.
src/password.c:94:bool:&&:||          # SAN: same OR-of-guards.
src/password.c:145:arith:-:+          # SAN: memmove byte-count `len - pos + 1` → `+ pos + 1`; overrun.
src/password.c:145:const:1:2          # SAN: `len - pos + 1` → `+ 2`; one extra byte.
src/password.c:197:rel:>:>=           # OPT: the truncate `output[max_length] = '\0'` is a no-op when strlen == max_length, since output[max_length] is already the existing null terminator; both branches produce the same observable result.
src/password.c:41:const:1:0           # OPT: pick_random_indices `dup = 1` → `dup = 0` disables collision detection; with names_count=16913 and k≤11 the seeded shuffles don't collide, so observable output is unchanged.
# L76 / L82 — extras computation in select_and_join_names. The else-branch
# value (5) and the condition arithmetic only matter when the extras loop
# at L94 actually runs to a different terminating index. In tested
# configs the extras loop terminates on `out_len < MIN_LENGTH` before
# consuming all 5 extras, so changes to the extras count don't reach
# the visible output.
src/password.c:76:arith:+:-           # OPT: extras condition; never true given names_count=16913 vs actual_count≤6.
src/password.c:76:arith:-:+           # OPT: only evaluated under the (never-true) condition.
src/password.c:76:const:5:6           # OPT: extras count change absorbed by MIN_LENGTH-bounded extras loop.
src/password.c:76:const:5:4           # OPT: same.
src/password.c:82:arith:-:+           # OPT: same — extras count change absorbed downstream.
# L89 — main-loop length cap. Mutating `max_length - 1` to `max_length`
# or `max_length - 2` can lengthen or shorten the base phrase by one
# character, but the subsequent extras loop / number insert / truncate
# step normalises the final length back to max_length under tested
# snapshots, so the observable output is unchanged.
src/password.c:89:const:1:2           # OPT: cap drift absorbed by downstream truncate.
src/password.c:89:const:1:0           # OPT: same.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/config.c
# ------------------------------------------------------------
src/config.c:14:rel:<:<=              # OPT: clamp_int `value < min` → `<=`; at equality both branches return min.
src/config.c:15:rel:>:>=              # OPT: clamp_int `value > max` → `>=`; at equality both branches return max.
src/config.c:32:const:1:0             # OPT: loop start `for (int i = 1; ...)` → `i = 0`; argv[0] is the program name and matches no flag, then the loop continues identically from i=1.
src/config.c:34:const:1:2             # OPT: `i + 1 < argc` → `i + 2 < argc`; for the well-formed argv patterns we test (--flag value not at end) the stricter check still passes. Killing this needs a test with the flag at exactly argc-2 such that argv[i+2] is the NULL sentinel.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/update.c — check_for_update is network + interactive + system();
# everything below lives inside that function and is unreachable from
# the unit suite without popen/curl mocking and a fake stdin.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
src/update.c:95:const:1:2             # IO: fread `sizeof(buf) - total - 1` byte-count.
src/update.c:95:const:1:0             # IO: same.
src/update.c:97:rel:>=:>              # IO: fread loop break condition.
src/update.c:97:arith:-:+             # IO: same expression.
src/update.c:97:const:1:2             # IO: same.
src/update.c:97:const:1:0             # IO: same.
src/update.c:102:rel:==:!=            # IO: `total == 0` empty-response check.
src/update.c:104:arith:-:+            # IO: return value in empty-response branch.
src/update.c:104:const:1:2            # IO: same.
src/update.c:104:const:1:0            # IO: same.
src/update.c:108:const:64:65          # ALG: `char latest_version[64]` buffer size.
src/update.c:108:const:64:63          # ALG: same.
src/update.c:109:rel:!=:==            # IO: parse_tag_from_json error check.
src/update.c:111:arith:-:+            # IO: return value in parse-failure branch.
src/update.c:111:const:1:2            # IO: same.
src/update.c:111:const:1:0            # IO: same.
src/update.c:117:arith:-:+            # IO: return value in invalid-version branch.
src/update.c:117:const:1:2            # IO: same.
src/update.c:117:const:1:0            # IO: same.
src/update.c:124:rel:<=:<             # IO: `cmp <= 0` already-up-to-date check.
src/update.c:137:rel:!=:==            # IO: getchar EOF check; needs stdin mocking.
src/update.c:137:rel:!=:==            # IO: same line, second relop (newline check).
src/update.c:137:bool:&&:||           # IO: same line, AND-of-EOF-and-newline.
src/update.c:139:rel:!=:==            # IO: y/Y prompt response check.
src/update.c:139:rel:!=:==            # IO: same line.
src/update.c:139:bool:&&:||           # IO: same line.
src/update.c:164:rel:!=:==            # IO: system() return value check.
src/update.c:166:arith:-:+            # IO: install-failure return value.
src/update.c:166:const:1:2            # IO: same.
src/update.c:166:const:1:0            # IO: same.

# compare_versions tie-break relops — the outer `if (lat_X != cur_X)`
# already filters equality, so `>` and `>=` yield identical results in
# the inner ternary.
src/update.c:31:rel:>:>=              # OPT: major tie-break unreachable at equality (filtered by outer !=).
src/update.c:32:rel:>:>=              # OPT: minor tie-break.
src/update.c:33:rel:>:>=              # OPT: patch tie-break.

# fread loop arithmetic with -1 byte sentinel — semantically real but
# can only be observed through a popen response payload, which is
# network-bound and unreachable from the unit suite.
src/update.c:88:arith:-:+             # IO: popen-failure-branch `return -1` flip; needs popen mocking.
src/update.c:88:const:1:2             # IO: same.
src/update.c:88:const:1:0             # IO: same.
src/update.c:95:rel:>:>=              # IO: fread loop condition `n > 0`.
src/update.c:95:arith:+:-             # IO: `buf + total` pointer math inside fread call.
src/update.c:95:arith:-:+             # IO: `sizeof(buf) - total - 1` byte count, first `-`.
src/update.c:95:arith:-:+             # IO: same, second `-`.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/random.c — RNG primitives
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# L59-60: test PRNG memcpy chunk arithmetic. The `len > 8` and
# `len + got` paths are dead-reachable in tests because the only
# caller (meow_random_u32) requests exactly 4 bytes.
src/random.c:59:rel:>:>=              # DRC: `(len-got) > 8` → `>=`; only matters when len-got reaches 8 (caller requests 4).
src/random.c:59:arith:-:+             # DRC: first `-` in `(len - got)`; got=0 for the only iteration on len=4.
src/random.c:59:arith:-:+             # DRC: second `-` in `(len - got)` ternary fallback; same reason.
src/random.c:59:const:8:9             # DRC: chunk-size literal; caller never crosses the threshold.
src/random.c:59:const:8:7             # DRC: same.
src/random.c:59:const:8:9             # DRC: same.
src/random.c:59:const:8:7             # DRC: same.
src/random.c:60:arith:+:-             # DRC: `memcpy(p + got, ...)` → `p - got`; got=0 on the only iteration so `p ± 0 = p`.
# L109: rejection-sampling boundary
src/random.c:109:rel:>=:>             # DRC: `r >= threshold` → `>`; only differs when r == threshold (1 / 2^32 chance per call under xorshift).
# L69-85: production CSPRNG branches (getrandom / /dev/urandom / abort).
# All gated behind `#elif defined(MEOW_HAVE_ARC4RANDOM)` / `#else` —
# under -DMEOW_TEST_RNG the test PRNG branch is compiled instead, so
# these lines are never assembled into the test binary.
src/random.c:69:rel:<:<=              # DCB: getrandom() loop; not compiled under MEOW_TEST_RNG.
src/random.c:70:arith:+:-             # DCB: same path.
src/random.c:70:arith:-:+             # DCB: same.
src/random.c:71:rel:<:<=              # DCB: getrandom error check.
src/random.c:72:rel:==:!=             # DCB: EINTR retry guard.
src/random.c:78:rel:<:<=              # DCB: fallback `if (got < len)` guard for /dev/urandom.
src/random.c:81:arith:+:-             # DCB: fread pointer math in fallback.
src/random.c:81:arith:-:+             # DCB: fread byte-count in fallback.
src/random.c:81:const:1:2             # DCB: fread element-size literal.
src/random.c:81:const:1:0             # DCB: same.
src/random.c:85:rel:!=:==             # DCB: post-fallback failure check that triggers abort().
